Framework · free to use

2026 fractional CMO & CRO pricing benchmark.

An observational pricing benchmark for fractional CMO, CRO, CFO, and COO engagements at B2B mid-market companies in May 2026. Built from Treetop's engagement observations, public job posts, and competitor pricing visibility. Citable numbers labeled by confidence level.

About this benchmark

Methodology and limits

What this is: An observational pricing benchmark for fractional CMO, CRO, CFO, and COO engagements at B2B mid-market companies ($3M-$50M ARR). Synthesized from Treetop's engagement work, conversations with peer firms, public job postings, and competitor pricing visibility, May 2026.

What this isn't: A formal survey. The fractional executive market is opaque by nature — rates vary by operator profile, engagement scope, and negotiation. Use these numbers as a starting point for budgeting and benchmarking, not as the floor or ceiling for any specific engagement.

Permission to cite: Yes, with attribution to Treetop Growth Strategy and a link to this page. Numbers are refreshed quarterly.

Fractional CMO pricing — May 2026

By company size

Company stageMedian monthly feeTypical rangeTypical time commitment
$3M-$10M ARR\$10,000\$8K-\$15K1 day/week
$10M-$25M ARR\$15,000\$12K-\$22K1-2 days/week
$25M-$50M ARR\$22,000\$18K-\$30K2-3 days/week

Profile premium: operators with deep vertical expertise (e.g., fintech, healthtech, devtools) typically command 10-25% premiums over generalist fractionals at the same time commitment.

Fractional CRO pricing — May 2026

By company size

Company stageMedian monthly feeTypical rangeTypical time commitment
$3M-$10M ARR\$12,000\$10K-\$18K1 day/week
$10M-$25M ARR\$18,000\$15K-\$25K1-2 days/week
$25M-$50M ARR\$25,000\$20K-\$35K2-3 days/week

Fractional CRO rates run ~15-20% higher than fractional CMO at the same company stage, reflecting cross-functional scope (sales + CS + RevOps) and higher accountability for revenue numbers.

Fractional CFO pricing — May 2026

By company size

Company stageMedian monthly feeTypical rangeTypical time commitment
$3M-$10M ARR\$8,000\$6K-\$12K1-2 days/week
$10M-$25M ARR\$12,000\$9K-\$18K2 days/week
$25M-$50M ARR\$16,000\$13K-\$22K2-3 days/week
Fractional COO / Chief of Staff pricing

By company size

Company stageMedian monthly feeTypical rangeTypical time commitment
$3M-$10M ARR\$9,000\$7K-\$14K1-2 days/week
$10M-$25M ARR\$14,000\$11K-\$20K2 days/week
$25M-$50M ARR\$18,000\$15K-\$25K2-3 days/week
What's typically included

Scope by tier

At \$8K-\$12K/month

  • Weekly working session with founder/CEO
  • Direction-setting and prioritization
  • Documented quarterly roadmap
  • Light executional support; mostly direction
  • Async check-ins between sessions

At \$12K-\$20K/month

  • All of the above, plus:
  • Direct work on highest-leverage initiatives
  • Team management (1-2 direct reports possible)
  • Vendor management
  • Quarterly business reviews owned by fractional

At \$20K+/month

  • All of the above, plus:
  • Full P&L responsibility for the function
  • Team management of 3-5 reports
  • Board reporting
  • Hiring leadership for the function (often including hiring the eventual full-time replacement)
Engagement structure norms

What's standard in 2026

  • Minimum commitment: 6 months. Most engagements run 12-18 months.
  • Notice period: 30 days standard. 60 days for senior CRO/CMO with team management responsibility.
  • Equity: uncommon at \$8K-\$15K/month; sometimes added at \$15K+/month, typically 0.1-0.5% over 2-year vest.
  • Performance bonus: uncommon for fractional CMOs; more common for fractional CROs tied to revenue metrics.
  • Travel/expenses: typically reimbursed; not built into the monthly fee.
Geographic variations

Where rates differ

  • Bay Area / NYC: typically 10-20% above the medians shown, reflecting talent cost.
  • Boston / Seattle / LA: rough parity with the medians shown.
  • Austin / Denver / Chicago / Atlanta: rough parity with medians; some operators discount 5-15% from coastal-market rates.
  • Remote / smaller markets: typically 5-15% below medians, though top-tier operators command coastal rates regardless of location.
What's driving 2026 pricing

Recent trends

  • Supply has grown faster than demand. Tech-sector talent disruption has put more senior operators into fractional roles. Rates have plateaued or softened modestly at the generalist tier.
  • Vertical specialists hold pricing. Fintech, healthtech, devtools, B2B SaaS specialists with named successful engagements command stable or rising rates.
  • AI-augmented fractional work compounds value per hour. Some fractional executives now do meaningfully more work per day than 2-3 years ago, putting modest upward pressure on per-day rates from top performers.
  • 'Builder' profile premiums rising. Buyers increasingly distinguish execution-capable fractionals from strategy-only fractionals, with willingness to pay 10-20% premiums for the former.
How to use these numbers

For buyers and operators

If you're a buyer

Use these as anchor points for negotiation. Don't expect to hire well-below-median; don't pay well-above-median without specific justification (vertical expertise, recent track record, network access). Always negotiate scope clarity before negotiating rate.

If you're a fractional operator

If your rates are well below median, you're either early-stage in fractional work or underselling. If above median, you should be able to articulate the specific edge — don't expect buyers to pay 30%+ premium without proof.

Methodology

Sources

  • Treetop's own engagement portfolio and pipeline conversations.
  • Direct conversations with ~15 peer fractional consultancies and individual operators.
  • Public LinkedIn job postings for fractional roles.
  • Competitor pricing pages (where publicly listed).
  • Buyer conversations during prospect calls.
  • Sample skews toward B2B SaaS and services; light coverage of consumer brands.
  • Last refresh: May 2026. Updated quarterly.
